prompt:
I am done debugging stringly-typed config. Our edge service reads an INI-ish file and today it's all raw HashMap<String,String> lookups with unwraps sprinkled around — last outage was literally `port = fast` taking a worker fleet down at 3am with a panic and no location info. I want a proper two-layer loader in this crate (rs-cfgparse), src/lib.rs, no serde, no crates — hand-rolled typed extraction with REAL error values: every single error carries a 1-based (line, col), character-counted. Acceptance tests are in tests/config.rs; tests/, Cargo.toml, and .gitignore are off limits.

Layer 1 — RawConfig::parse(src: &str) -> Result<RawConfig, ParseError>. Format: lines are blank (skip), comments ('#' as the first non-blank character — a '#' inside a value is DATA), section headers `[name]` (optional indentation; '[' position is the section's position; missing ']' = UnclosedSection at the '[' col; non-space text after ']' = TrailingChars at that char's col), or `key = value` (split on the FIRST '='; key and value trimmed of spaces/tabs). No '=' on a non-header line, or an empty key = BadLine at the line's first non-blank col. Empty value = MissingValue at the '=' col. A key before any section header = KeyOutsideSection at the key's col. The same key twice in one section = DuplicateKey(key) at the SECOND occurrence's key col; reopening a section later is legal and accumulates keys (duplicates still collide across reopenings). Tabs count as one column. Tolerate CRLF (strip a trailing \r per line). RawConfig exposes get(&self, section, key) -> Option<&RawValue> where pub struct RawValue { pub text: String, pub line: usize, pub col: usize } (col = first char of the trimmed value), and section_pos(&self, name) -> Option<(usize, usize)> (the '[' position).

Layer 2 — pub fn load_server_config(src: &str) -> Result<ServerConfig, ConfigError> extracting section [server] into pub struct ServerConfig { pub host: String, pub port: u16, pub tls: bool, pub workers: u32, pub log_level: LogLevel, pub allow: Vec<String> } with pub enum LogLevel { Error, Warn, Info, Debug }. Field order host, port, tls, workers, log_level, allow; host and port required, the rest default to false / 4 / Info / empty vec. Rules: integers are optional-'-' then digits (anything else: WrongType expected "integer"), port must land in 1..=65535 and workers in 1..=512 or it's BadValue with message "port must be between 1 and 65535" / "workers must be between 1 and 512" (a digits-only value too big for i64 also gets the range message, never a panic); tls is exactly "true"/"false" (WrongType expected "boolean" otherwise — "True" doesn't fly); log_level is exactly one of error/warn/info/debug else BadValue "unknown log level \"<got>\""; allow splits on ',' with per-item trim, any empty item = BadValue "empty entry in allow list". Unknown keys are ignored. Positions: WrongType/BadValue point at the VALUE; a Missing key points at its section's '[' header; a missing [server] section reports the FIRST field in extraction order (host) at (1,1).

Error architecture — this is the part I care about, get it exactly right: pub struct ParseError { pub line, pub col, pub kind: ParseKind } with pub enum ParseKind { BadLine, UnclosedSection, TrailingChars, MissingValue, DuplicateKey(String), KeyOutsideSection }; pub struct FieldError { pub line, pub col, pub section: String, pub key: String, pub kind: FieldKind } with pub enum FieldKind { Missing, WrongType { expected: &'static str, got: String }, BadValue(String) }; pub enum ConfigError { Parse(ParseError), Field(FieldError) } with impl From<ParseError> and impl From<FieldError> (extraction code should be using `?` through those), accessor methods line()/col() on ConfigError, Display + std::error::Error on ConfigError rendering "line L, col C: ..." (field errors as "line L, col C: section.key: ..." — exact strings are pinned in the tests). Everything derives Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq (plus Copy where it's free, e.g. LogLevel).

Done = cargo test --offline green.
